1st June 2026: Laporan Pilihanraya Umum Malaysia 1995, New Straits Times 21st November 1999, Almanak keputusan pilihan raya umum : Parlimen & Dewan Undangan Negeri, 1959-1999 . For specific DUN Candidate background details, refer to 1995_Other_Sources_DUN_File

Parliamentary Constituency Names follow the EC Map of 1994. DAP pulled out Gagasan Rakyat on 25th January 1995 (few months before elections) and opted to maintain cooperation with S46 and remaining members of Gagasan Rakyat. S46 is a member of Gagasan Rakyat and Angkatan Perpaduan Ummah. EC and Tindak Malaysia computations doesn't agree on the total electorate, total ballots issued, total rejected votes and party votes (refer to table below for the discrepancies). EC errorneously combined UMNO and PAS candidates names of DUN Sungai Tiang (Kedah) which creates confusion for readers. Total party votes for S46 and AKIM for Kelantan has contradictions. If one uses EC individual returns for S46 and AKIM and tally them up, the total values would be 82401 (S46) and 1195 (AKIM). According to Almanak keputusan pilihan raya umum: Parlimen & Dewan Undangan Negeri, 1959 - 1999, the total party votes would be 82451 (S46) and 1145 (AKIM). EC made a mistake for winning majority for DUN Lembah Jaya (Selangor) where EC reports the majority as 13564 while the correct value is 13413.
